patches.find: the 3:1 is read directly off CHAOS-7 and copied into the WIN-063 paragraph as if that act of reading were itself a derivation
patches.replace: the dome asserts 3:1 without citing any source for this specific ratio — the dome's own paragraph frames 3:1 as a structural prediction of toroidal asymmetric flow, not as a value read from CHAOS-7; no derivation from dome geometry produces specifically 3:1 rather than any other ratio
patches.rationale: Hole 1 fix: dome does not cite CHAOS-7 as source for 3:1; our text was a strawman. Revised to accurately represent dome's claim (structural prediction, unsourced) while preserving the refutation that no geometry produces it.

patches.find: but adopts that ratio from CHAOS-7 observation rather than deriving it from dome geometry
patches.replace: but provides no derivation from dome geometry for this specific value — the 3:1 figure appears without a cited source or formula
patches.rationale: Hole 1 fix (second occurrence): remove false CHAOS-7 attribution; accurate description is that the dome provides no source or derivation at all.

patches.find: this is the same pattern that earned WIN-041 and WIN-042 "Misleading" verdicts for re-slicing decay statistics from the same dataset.
patches.replace: this is the same re-slicing pattern identified in WIN-041 and WIN-042 — where decay statistics from the same underlying dataset are repackaged as independent observations rather than derived from a new mechanism.
patches.rationale: Hole 2 fix: remove implication that WIN-063 should share the Misleading verdict; framing is now pattern-similarity without verdict equivalence. WIN-063 remains Std Model Explains (the dome's 3:1 is explained by standard MHD, not merely Misleading).

patches.find: no element of the published dome geometry breaks longitudinal symmetry
patches.replace: no element of the published dome geometry (the four canonical scalar parameters and the axisymmetric H(r) function) breaks longitudinal symmetry — dynamic conditions such as the sun's instantaneous position or transient aetheric flow structure could break azimuthal symmetry moment-to-moment, but these are not part of the dome's static geometric specification and the dome's WIN-063 claim is a structural prediction, not a time-tagged observation
patches.rationale: Hole 3 fix: add hedge distinguishing published static geometry from dynamic conditions, pre-empting the sun/moon position objection while preserving the core geometric argument.

patches.replace: (b) over what averaging window (the dome's claim is a structural ratio, not a time-varying rate — specifying the temporal averaging window is what makes 3:1 a testable number),
patches.rationale: Hole 4 fix: change 'over what time period' to 'over what averaging window' to match the nature of a ratio claim rather than a rate claim.
